snapshot:
You came in naming the afternoon energy crash, a wish for lower cholesterol, and a preference for halal, pescatarian-leaning cooking with South Asian and Mediterranean staples. Without a lab panel in hand, this first plan is written from your intake alone — it lines up the food moves and habits that hold up well for an adult of your stated age, sex, and dietary pattern, and it names the markers that would sharpen the next pass once you upload your labs.

The eat list pulls from foods that pair naturally with the cuisines you cook in; the avoid list reaches for the items that most reliably blunt afternoon energy. The habit stack is the easy tier — five small things a tired person can hold without thinking.

insights.title: Afternoon energy crash — the usual suspects
insights.detail: Without labs we cannot point to a single marker, but the pattern (post-lunch dip, return mid-afternoon) most often tracks with carbohydrate quality at lunch, hydration through the morning, and short sun exposure on waking. The plan below addresses all three.

insights.title: Cholesterol-friendly eating, written from intake
insights.detail: Until we see a lipid panel, the plan leans on the food moves with the strongest evidence base across a generic adult: soluble fiber daily, fatty fish a few times a week, leafy greens, and reducing refined seed oils. Lipid-specific tuning waits for labs.

insights.title: Halal pescatarian-leaning, South Asian + Mediterranean
insights.detail: Your dietary pattern carries the plan — every eat item below has a halal, pescatarian-friendly form, and the staples lean on cuisines you already cook in.

eatList.why: Omega-3s for the lipid profile and a real vitamin D source — both useful generic moves while we wait on labs.

eatList.portion: 1/2 cup oats OR 1 cup cooked lentils OR 1 tbsp psyllium
eatList.why: Pulls cholesterol out via bile-acid binding — the most direct dietary lever before we have a lipid panel to point at.
Folate, magnesium, and plant nitrates carry weight across cardiovascular and energy pathways; cook them in ghee or olive oil for absorption.
Steady protein at breakfast or snack — flattens the afternoon energy crash by smoothing morning blood sugar.

eatList.food: Extra-virgin olive oil as the daily fat
eatList.why: Replaces refined seed oils for cooking and finishing; backbone of the Mediterranean pattern.

avoidList.why: Pro-inflammatory and tied to higher cardiovascular risk markers across most adult populations.
avoidList.swap: Olive oil, avocado oil, or ghee

avoidList.food: Refined grains (white bread, white pasta, pastries) at lunch
avoidList.why: The fastest path to the afternoon energy crash you described.
avoidList.swap: Whole oats, intact grains, or legumes

avoidList.food: Sweetened drinks (sodas, mango lassi, sweet chai)
avoidList.why: Concentrated sugar undercuts both the energy and cholesterol goals.
avoidList.swap: Sparkling water with lime, unsweetened chai, plain yogurt lassi

lifestyle.title: 10-minute walk after lunch
lifestyle.why: Improves post-meal glucose and is the single highest-leverage move against the afternoon energy crash.
lifestyle.how: Set the alarm; walk the block once.
lifestyle.expectedImpact: Steadier afternoons within a week or two.

lifestyle.title: 10 minutes morning sun on skin
lifestyle.why: Anchors the circadian rhythm; supports endogenous vitamin D synthesis.
lifestyle.how: Within an hour of waking; arms and face, no sunscreen for that window.

habitStack.intro: Five small actions that compound across the week.

habits.title: 16 oz water on rising
habits.why: Hydration sets up steadier morning energy.

habits.title: 1/2 cup oats with breakfast
habits.cue: First thing, on the stove or microwave
habits.why: Soluble fiber daily, with morning protein.

habits.title: 10-min morning sun
habits.why: Endogenous vitamin D synthesis and circadian anchoring.

habits.title: Cooked greens at one meal
habits.why: Hits the leafy-greens target across the week.

retest.reason: Upload your most recent labs to sharpen this plan — a lipid panel and a vitamin D level would let the next pass name specific markers instead of writing from intake alone.
